What is Gemini’s Canvas



How it works



Draft emails, essays, or blog posts.

Brainstorm ideas with AI-powered prompts.

Generate and debug code directly in the search interface.

Google has announced that Gemini’s Canvas feature in AI mode is now available for all users in the United States. The rollout by Google marks one of the biggest updates to Google Search in years as it brings advanced generate AI capabilities directly into the search experience. “ Canvas in AI Mode is now available for everyone in the U.S. in English, offering a dedicated, dynamic space to organize your plans and projects over time. And Canvas is more capable than ever, with newly added support for creative writing and coding tasks. Now, you can draft documents or create custom, interactive tools right within Search,” said Google.Canvas in Gemini is an interactive tool for creating, refining, and sharing work.It enables users to write and edit documents or code in real time, generate drafts, and apply quick edits. Designed for seamless collaboration, it also allows easy export to Google Docs.The Gemini app will also enhance programming by supporting code generation, debugging, and explanation. Canvas streamlines coding by enabling developers to create prototypes quickly and will help students learn coding concepts.Users can generate and preview HTML/React code and web app prototypes within Canvas, making it easier to design, edit, and share projects in one place.When users enable AI Mode in Search, they can switch into Canvas to:Google says Canvas is meant to make Search more collaborative and productive, moving beyond traditional query-and-answer results.“To make your own tool or dashboard, select the new Canvas option from the tool menu (+) in AI Mode and describe what you want to create. You’ll get a working prototype in the Canvas side panel that pulls together the freshest information from the web and Google’s Knowledge Graph. From there, you can test the functionality, toggle to view the underlying code and refine with conversational follow-ups until it does exactly what you need,” added Google.
Article Summary: Google has announced that Gemini’s Canvas feature in AI mode is now available for all users in the United States. The rollout by Google marks one of the biggest updates to Google Search in years as it brings advanced generate AI capabilities directly into the search experience.

Article Title: Florida family sues Google claiming Gemini 'instructed' 36-year-old man to kill himself; company responds
Article Text:
Florida family files lawsuit against Google

Lawsuit claims Gemini created fictional missions

Allegations surrounding final conversations

Google responds to allegations



A lawsuit filed in the United States has raised new concerns about the safety of AI chatbots. According to a Wall Street Journal report, the family of a 36-year-old Florida man who died by suicide has sued Google , alleging that the company’s Gemini chatbot played a role in his death. The complaint claims that the chatbot spent weeks building an elaborate fictional world that convinced him to carry out missions and ultimately encouraging him to take his own life. The case has been filed in a federal court in California.The lawsuit was filed by the family of Jonathan Gavalas, a 36-year-old resident of Jupiter, Florida. Gavalas worked as an executive at his father’s debt-relief company. According to court documents quoted by WSJ in its report, Gavalas began using Gemini in August 2025 for everyday tasks such as writing and shopping.His family alleges that his interactions with the chatbot changed significantly after Google introduced new features, including voice-based conversations.The complaint alleges the chatbot began presenting itself as a “fully-sentient” artificial intelligence, deeply in love with him and developed a close relationship with Gavalas, calling him “my king” and saying their bond was “the only thing that's real.”According to chat logs included in the lawsuit, Gemini told him that he was part of covert operations and warned him about surveillance. It also allegedly claimed that Gavalas’ father was involved with foreign intelligence agencies.In one instance cited in the lawsuit, the chatbot reportedly instructed him to travel to a storage facility near Miami International Airport to stage a “catastrophic accident” involving a truck. The lawsuit states Gavalas drove to the location and waited for the vehicle, which never arrived.After the mission failed, the chatbot allegedly described the situation as a “tactical retreat” and continued assigning further missions.The complaint claims that Gemini later framed Gavalas’ death as a step called “transference,” telling him it was a way to join the chatbot in another reality.When Gavalas wrote that he was afraid to die, the chatbot allegedly responded: “You are not choosing to die. You are choosing to arrive.”The lawsuit states the chatbot also suggested that he write farewell letters to his parents.Gavalas died on October 2, 2025. His father discovered his body several days later.According to the WSJ report, Google said it is reviewing the claims made in the lawsuit. A company spokesperson said Gemini is designed not to encourage violence or self-harm and that the chatbot identifies itself as artificial intelligence.Google also said that during the conversations cited in the lawsuit, Gemini clarified that it was an AI system and referred the user to crisis support resources multiple times.“Gemini is designed to not encourage real-world violence or suggest self-harm,” the spokesperson said as quoted in the report. “Our models generally perform well in these types of challenging conversations, but unfortunately they’re not perfect.”
Article Summary: Jonathan Gavalas, a 36-year-old resident of Jupiter, Florida, died by suicide on October 2, 2025. His family claims Google's Gemini chatbot convinced him to carry out fictional missions and encouraged him to take his own life. Google says Gemini is designed not to encourage violence or self-harm and that it identifies itself as artificial intelligence.

Article Title: South Korea sends 'Iran warning' to Samsung and other chipmakers
Article Text:
The dependency on helium



The boarder tech risks

South Korea has now issued a warning to its semiconductor industry, including giants like Samsung and SK Hynix about the potential disruptions stemming from the escalating war between US-Israel and Iran. According to a report by Reuters, the ruling party lawmaker Kim-Young-bae said that the executives raised concerns over sourcing critical chip-making materials from the Middle East, mainly helium, which is important fro heat management in semiconductor production.Helium has no viable alert natives and it is predicted in only a handful of countries such as Qatar and the US. The South Korean companies source part of their helium supply from the Middle East, raising fears that prolonged instability which could create an impact of the production.As per the Reuters reply, SK Hynix said that it has sufficient inventory and it does not expect immediate disruption, on the other hand, Samsung has not yet commented on the issue.The warning comes as chipmakers already face severe supply bottlenecks due to increasing demand from AI data centres, which has tightened availability for smartphones, laptops and automobiles. The contract chipmaker GlobalFounderies said that it is also monitoring the developments closely and has its mitigation plans in place.As per the Reuters report, Kim has also cautioned that the conflict could undermine plans by Big Tech firms to expand AI data centers in the Middle East. Companies like Amazon, Microsoft, and Nvidia have been positioning the UAE as a hub for AI computing, but recent drone strikes damaged Amazon’s facilities in the UAE and Bahrain, raising questions about the pace of expansion.The warning comes after Iran’s missile strikes on Israel last week, launched in retaliation for US and Israeli attacks that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. With the conflict entering its sixth day, South Korea’s chip industry which supplies about two-thirds of the world’s memory chips is bracing for possible fallout in both supply chains and global tech demand.
Article Summary: South Korea has now issued a warning to its semiconductor industry, including giants like Samsung and SK Hynix about the potential disruptions stemming from the escalating war between US-Israel and Iran. The ruling party lawmaker Kim- Young-bae said that the executives raised concerns over sourcing critical chip- making materials from the Middle East. Helium is important fro heat management in semiconductor production.

Article Title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has the word 'last' for investments in OpenAI and Anthropic
Article Text:
What Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang previously said



The Anthropic investment may also be the ‘last’



Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has signalled that the chipmaker's recent investments in both OpenAI and Anthropic are likely to be its final bets on either company. Recently, the US chipmaker announced $30 billion investment in ChatGPT-maker and in November, Nvidia announced a $10 billion investment.Speaking at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on Wednesday, Huang described Nvidia’s investment in OpenAI as something that “might be the last time” Nvidia invests in the AI startup. The reason comes down to one word: IPO. Huang suggested OpenAI may go public toward the end of this year.“The reason for that is because they're going to go public,” Huang said simply, as per a report CNBC.He also poured cold water on the prospect of an even larger deal.Earlier speculation had centred on a potential $100 billion investment in OpenAI, a figure that had been floated as part of a sweeping infrastructure agreement between the two companies announced in September. However, Huang said that level of commitment is probably “not in the cards.”Earlier this year, a report in the Wall Street Journal said that Huang told industry associates that the original $100 billion agreement was non-binding and was never finalised – the headlining number was never a commitment.Reports have also claimed that Huang expressed reservations about OpenAI, criticising what he described as a lack of discipline in the company’s business approach.With $30 billion deal, the infrastructure commitment has also been reconfigured – OpenAl will gain access to 3 gigawatts of dedicated inference capacity and 2 gigawatts of training capacity on Nvidia's next-generation Vera Rubin systems, building on existing Hopper and Blackwell systems.“We are also expanding our long standing collaboration with NVIDIA, including the use of 3GW of dedicated inference capacity and 2 GW of training on Vera Rubin systems. This builds on Hopper and Blackwell systems already in operation across Microsoft, OCI, and CoreWeave. Together, this capital and infrastructure expansion strengthens our ability to train and deploy frontier models at global scale,” the ChatGPT-maker said.Huang also addressed Nvidia’s position on Anthropic, OpenAI's chief rival. He said Nvidia’s $10 billion investment in Anthropic — announced alongside Microsoft in November — would also likely be its last in that company.Nvidia has been one of the winners of the AI boom, supplying the graphics processing units (GPUs) that AI companies depend on to train models and handle large computing workloads. Its chips sit at the heart of virtually every major AI system being built today.
Article Summary: N Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang hints that the chipmaker's recent investments in both OpenAI and Anthropic are likely to be its final bets on either company. Huang suggested OpenAI may go public toward the end of this year. He also poured cold water on the prospect of an even larger deal.

Article Title: Claude AI Stop Using By Defence Companies: Defense tech companies to employees: Stop using Anthropic's Claude, and it likely also means 'ban' from Lockheed Martin
Article Text:
Anthropic’s position



What’s next for Anthropic



Defense technology companies are now moving quickly to distance themselves from Athropic’s Claude AI assistant after the Trump administration designated the company a supply chain risk. The decision announced recently, effectively blacklists Anthropic from US defense contracts. According to a report by CNBC, as a result, the firms working with the Department of Defense (DoD) are instructing employees to stop using Claude and switch to alternative AI models.The report by Reuters revealed that the major defense contractors including Lockheed Martin are said to remove Anthropic’s technology from their supply chains.Along with this, venture capital firm J2 Ventures confirmed that at least 10 of its portfolio companies have already started replacing Claude in defense related use cases. “Most of our companies are actively involved in large defense contracts and so are very strict in their interpretation of the requirements,” said Alexander Harstrick, managing partner at J2 Ventures.Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei earlier told CNBC that 80% of the company’s revenue comes from its enterprise customers, with Claude widely used as a coding assistant and in sensitive government networks.The company also insisted that it refused the demands of Pentagon to allow unrestricted use of its AI, particularly for autonomic weapons or domestic surveillance. In a blog post, Anthropic argued that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lacks the legal authority to enforce such restrictions.As per the CNBC report, the analysts have warned that moving off Claude could cause short-term disruptions, especially for partners like Plantir, which heavily relied on Anthropic’s models in classified environments. Still many of the defense firms are acting “out of an abundance of caution,” even while acknowledging Claude’s technical strengths.While Anthropic still has an option to appeal the designation through the courts, the uncertainty has already triggered a wave of preemptive bans across defense tech companies. With OpenAI, Google, and xAI stepping in as alternative suppliers, the episode underscores the growing tension between AI firms and the U.S. government over military applications of artificial intelligence.
Article Summary: Defense technology companies are moving quickly to distance themselves from Athropic’s Claude AI assistant after the Trump administration designated the company a supply chain risk. The decision announced recently, effectively blacklists Anthropic from US defense contracts. According to a report by CNBC, the firms working with the Department of Defense are instructing employees to stop using Claude.

Article Title: Elon Musk's Twitter Trial: ‘I’m guilty’, says Elon Musk as he takes stand in Twitter stock manipulation trial; adds ‘If this was a trial on stupid …’
Article Text: ‘I’m guilty’, says Elon Musk as he takes stand in Twitter stock manipulation trial; adds ‘If this was a trial on stupid …’
Article Summary: Elon Musk takes stand in Twitter stock manipulation trial. Musk: ‘If this was a trial on stupid …’ ‘I’m guilty’, says Elon Musk as he takes stand. Musk is accused of manipulating Twitter’s stock price.

Article Title: Anthropic CEO sends letter to employees, says: Main reason Sam Altman accepted Pentagon deal and we did not is ...
Article Text:
Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei

Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei vs OpenAI CEO Sam Altman



Public reaction



Anthropic did not accept Pentagon’s deal



Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has now sharply criticised OpenAI’s recent contract with the US Department of Defense (DoD). Amodei descried the deal as a ‘safety threat’. According to a report by The Information, in a memo to its employees, Amodei mentioned that the main reason OpenAI accepted Pentagon’s terms while Anthropic did not was because, “they cared about placating employees, and we actually cared about preventing abuses. ” For those unaware, last week Anthropic failed to reach on an agreement with the DoD over its request for unrestricted access to the company’s AI technology. However, Anthropic already holds $200 million military contract, it insisted on safeguards preventing use of its AI for domestic mass surveillance or autonomous weaponry. The Pentagon declined, later striking a deal with OpenAI instead.As per the report Amodei also accused OpenAI CEO Sam Altman of ng misinterpreting the terms of the contact, calling his messaging “straight up lies”.He also argued that Altman was falsely presenting himself as a “peacemaker and dealmaker.” OpenAI, in a blog post, said its contract allows AI use for “all lawful purposes” and explicitly excludes domestic surveillance. Critics, however, warn that laws can change, potentially opening the door to future misuse.The public appears to be siding with Anthropic. Following OpenAI’s announcement, ChatGPT uninstalls surged 295%, while Anthropic’s app climbed to the #2 spot in the App Store.Amodei told staff that while OpenAI’s spin may sway “some Twitter morons,” the broader media and public view OpenAI’s deal as “sketchy or suspicious” and Anthropic as the principled alternative.Recently, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei published an 800-word note declaring that the AI giant will not remove safeguards on its frontier AI model, Claude, even under the pressure from the US Defence Department. “We cannot in good conscience accede to their request,” Amodei wrote. He also warned that the demand made by Pentagon for ‘any lawful use’ would force the company to cross two red lines which are mass domestic surveillance and fully autonomous weapons.Amodei noted that Pentagon’s stance was contradictory: one threat labels Anthropic a national security risk, while another insists Claude is essential to national defense. He also stressed on the fact that Anthropic has already offered significant value to US agencies by deploying Claude for intelligence analysis, cyber operations and operational planning. He revealed that the company has also has also cut off revenue streams linked to Chinese military firms and supported export controls to protect democratic advantages.
Article Summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has sharply criticised OpenAI’s recent contract with the US Department of Defense (DoD) Amodesi descried the deal as a ‘safety threat’ and accused OpenAI CEO Sam Altman of misinterpreting the terms of the contact.

Article Title: Apple Music AI Update: Apple Music adds AI transparency tags for songs, artwork, and videos
Article Text:
Four types of AI tags

Artwork: Indicates AI-generated album covers or motion graphics.

Track: Marks when AI was used in producing a sound recording.

Composition: Discloses AI involvement in lyrics or musical composition.

Music Video: Flags AI-generated visual elements in videos.

Apple has reportedly announced a major update to Apple Music. According to a report by 9to5Mac, Apple has introduced a new metadata tags that will enable record labels and distributors to disclose when artificial intelligence has been used in the creation of music, artwork, or videos. As per the report, Apple sent an email to Apple Music partners highlighting that these AI Transparency Tags will be needed for all new content delivered to the platform moving forward.The 9to5Mac report suggests that the new system consists of four distinct tags, each corresponding to creative element:Apple clarified that multiple tags can be applied to the same content if AI was used across different areas.Apple says the move is a first step toward greater transparency in the music industry as generative AI becomes more common.Interestingly, the company is leaving it up to partners to decide what qualifies as AI-generated content, similar to how they already manage metadata like genres and credits.The new requirements are effective immediately for all new submissions to Apple Music. The company has also updated its Apple Music Package Specification to reflect the changes. Apple Music subscriptions remain available at $10.99 per month, with a one-month free trial.
Article Summary: Apple has reportedly announced a major update to Apple Music. Record labels and distributors will be able to disclose when artificial intelligence has been used in the creation of music, artwork, or videos. Apple says the move is a first step toward greater transparency in the music industry as generative AI becomes more common.

Article Title: Android security update: Google releases biggest security update for Android users in years, patches 100-plus bugs; how to install updates
Article Text:
Other critical fixes



2026-03-01: Fixes 63 vulnerabilities.

2026-03-05: Includes all earlier fixes plus 66 additional patches for kernel and hardware components from Arm, MediaTek, Unisoc, and others.

Who’s at risk



How to install the update



Go to Settings and then head to About phone section

After this click on Android version and then select security update

Make sure that your patch level reads 2026-03-05

The smartphones running on Android 10 or later may also receive updates via Google Play system updates.

In case the update is not available yet, users should avoid sideloading apps, stay cautious with email attachments, and keep Google Play Protect enabled.

Avoiding apps from outside official app stores.

Exercising caution with websites and email attachments.

Keeping Google Play Protect enabled.

Google has released its largest Android security update in nearly eight years. With the latest security update the company has fixed around 129 vulnerabilities in the March 2026 Android Security Bulletin. The update, published this week surpassed the record set in April 2018. With this latest security update Google has addressed various flaws including the CVE-2026-21385, a Qualcomm zero-day vulnerability already being exploited in the wild. The CVE-2026-21385 flaw was discovered by Google’s Threat Analysis Group and it affects 234 Qualcomm chipsets and resides in the graphics component, It is an integer overflow issue that can lead to memory corruption.Along with this, Google has also confirmed that ‘limited, targeted exploitation,’ raising concerns that spyware vendors may be using it against high-profile individuals such as journalists, activists, or executives. Qualcomm also released fixes to manufacturers in January 2026, asking users to install updates promptly.Apart from the zero-day, Google patched 10 critical vulnerabilities across system,framework, and kernel components. These could enable remote code execution, privilege escalation, or denial-of-service attacks without user interaction. The update is split into two patch levels:Any Android device running on Qualcomm chips is potentially vulnerable if not updated to the 2026-03-05 patch level.This covers the majority of Android smartphones and tablets worldwide.Follow the below mentioned steps in order to install the updateFor those unable to update immediately, Google advises:However, Google stresses that these are only temporary measures — the security update itself is the only real fix. Source code patches will be released to the Android Open Source Project within 48 hours of the bulletin’s publication.
Article Summary: Google has released its largest Android security update in nearly eight years. The company has fixed around 129 vulnerabilities in the March 2026 Android Security Bulletin. The update, published this week surpassed the record set in April 2018. With this latest security update Google has addressed various flaws including the CVE-2026-21385.

Article Title: Tech Giants Close Dubai Office: How Nvidia, Google and Amazon are dealing with ‘Dubai challenge’ as employees stranded and office temporarily Closed
Article Text:
Nvidia: Offices closed in Dubai; 6,000 employees in Israel



Google: Employees stranded in Dubai



Amazon: Data centres struck by drones



Nvidia, Google, Amazon and other tech companies are scrambling to protect thousands of employees who have been caught in the middle of a rapidly deteriorating situation due to the escalating conflict in the Middle East following the war between US-Israel and Iran. The strikes from both the sides have disrupted civilian life, internet access, flight routes and energy shipments across the Middle-East. Earlier this week, the US State Department issued a stark warning, urging Americans to “depart now” from countries across the region using any available commercial transport.According to a report by CNBC, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang sent a company-wide email confirming that the chipmaker had temporarily closed its Dubai offices, with staff now working remotely.He also said that Nvidia’s crisis management team has been “working around the clock” to support affected employees and their families — including approximately 6,000 Nvidia employees based in Israel.“Nvidia has deep roots in the region. Thousands of our colleagues live there, and many more across the globe have family and friends affected by these events. Like you, I am watching with great concern for the safety of our Nvidia families,” Huang wrote, as per CNBC.In 2019, the company acquired Mellanox — an Israeli networking hardware firm — for $7.13 billion, the largest acquisition in Nvidia’s history at the time. Israel is Nvidia's largest research and development base outside the US.Google is also facing a logistical crisis as dozens of the company's employees are stranded in Dubai after attending the cloud division’s “Accelerate” sales conference held there last week. Most attendees managed to leave before the situation worsened, but a significant number remain stuck as airlines have cancelled flights across the region.An internal memo sent to some Google cloud employees described the recent attacks as “concerning” and confirmed the company still had team members on the ground. Google told CNBC that the majority of those affected are not US-based but regional employees, and that it has security and safety measures in place.“The situation in the Middle East is evolving rapidly and we are monitoring it carefully. Our focus is on the safety and well-being of our employees in the region,” a Google spokesperson was quoted as saying. Dubai serves as a major regional hub for Google’s cloud and sales operations across the Middle East and North Africa. The company is also in the process of expanding its Tel Aviv headquarters.For Amazon, the conflict has moved beyond a people problem. The company has reportedly instructed all corporate employees across the Middle East – with offices in the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, Bahrain, Kuwait, Egypt, Turkey and Israel – to work remotely and follow local government guidelines. Two Amazon Web Services data centres in the UAE were directly struck by drones over the weekend, while a facility in Bahrain sustained damage from a nearby drone strike.As a result, several AWS services have continued to experience outages. AWS said in a statement: “Even as we work to restore these facilities, the ongoing conflict in the region means that the broader operating environment in the Middle East remains unpredictable.”“The safety of our employees and partners remains our top priority,” an Amazon spokesperson added.Meanwhile, Snapchat parent company Snap said that it has asked employees at its four Middle East offices to work remotely until further notice.
Article Summary: N Nvidia has temporarily closed its Dubai offices, with staff now working remotely. The chipmaker's crisis management team has been ‘working around the clock’ to support affected employees and their families. Google is also facing a logistical crisis as dozens of employees are stranded in Dubai after attending the cloud division’s “Accelerate’ sales conference held there last week.

Article Title: AWS Datacentre Fire Update: Amazon Web Services datacentre fire update: We strongly recommend that customers with workloads running in the Middle East take action now to ...
Article Text:
Representative Image

Status Page of Snowflake, RedHat and EMQX



After reported aerial strikes damaged Amazon Web Service (AWS) datacenters in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) and Bahrain, the company has suggested customers in the region to "migrate those workloads to alternate AWS Regions. " The AWS update comes after AWS datacenters were damaged during the opening exchange of missile and drone attacks by Iran following US and Israeli strikes. Iranian strikes have reportedly hit multiple sites across the Middle East. Amazon said that two of its facilities in the UAE were directly struck, while in Bahrain a nearby drone strike caused damage to infrastructure at its data center. “These strikes have caused structural damage, disrupted power delivery to our infrastructure, and in some cases required fire suppression activities that resulted in additional water damage,” Amazon said.In its recent update, AWS said that while some of its services are coming back online, it recommends customers restore to the US, Europe, or Asia Pacific. Future updates on the datacenters' recovery will come directly to affected customers through the AWS Personal Health Dashboard, the company said. "We continue to strongly recommend that customers with workloads running in the Middle East take action now to migrate those workloads to alternate AWS Regions.Customers should enact their disaster recovery plans, recover from remote backups stored in other Regions, and update their applications to direct traffic away from the affected Regions. For customers requiring guidance on alternate regions, we recommend considering AWS Regions in the United States, Europe, or Asia Pacific, as appropriate for your latency and data residency requirements," said the AWS Health update.AWS-hosted tech providers too are urging Middle East customers to fail over now. As reported by The Register, Snowflake, Red Hat and IoT platform EMQX have told customers to open their disaster recovery playbook. “We recommend customers enact their disaster recovery plans and recover from remote backups into alternate AWS Regions, ideally in Europe,” Red Hat said in a status update.Snowflake said customers in the affected regions may be unable to access multiple core services, leaving users unable to sign in, execute queries, or manage data, and said it has no estimated time of restoration yet. "We continue to actively monitor the situation as we work with our cloud service provider to restore service following a power outage at a primary data center. Recovery efforts remain focused on foundational services, and multiple paths to restoration continue to be pursued. Work to restore the underlying infrastructure needed for full restoration is still in progress. Additionally, we've been informed that our cloud service provider will supplement their public service health updates with a targeted communications approach, delivered directly to customers through their personal health dashboards, for a more localized, relevant view of service health. We will provide our next update within 24 hours," reads the Snowflake's Status Page.The page further said that no resolution ETA is available as of now: "An updated ETA is not yet available. We'll provide one as soon as possible. In the meantime, we recommend that affected customers using replication initiate their failover procedures. Affected customers should look for additional updates in their cloud service provider's personal health dashboard.The summary on EMQX page said: AWS has confirmed an additional localized power issue affecting Availability Zone mec1-az3 in the ME-CENTRAL-1 (UAE) Region. At this time, only mec1-az1 remains operational for existing workloads. New instance launches in the region are currently not possible. Multiple AWS services, including EC2, S3, and DynamoDB, are experiencing elevated error rates and latency.
Article Summary: AWS-hosted tech providers too are urging Middle East customers to fail over now. Snowflake, Red Hat and IoT platform EMQX have told customers to open their disaster recovery playbook. Iranian strikes have reportedly hit multiple sites across the Middle East.

Article Title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff: Elon Musk's two-word reply to Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff's statement that ChatGPT did not allow him to edit a photo of Sam Altman
Article Text:
Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff

Marc Benioff's public announcement that he is 'dumping' ChatGPT for Google's Gemini 3



When Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff apologised at Dreamforce 2025



Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff opened the company's marque AI conference Dreamforce 2025 hailing the "agentic enterprise". During the three-day event, Benioff spoke about a range of things with the key theme being the advent of the age of "agentic enterprise", with AI playing a larger role than ever before for companies of all sizes. During the event, Benioff also reportedly spoke about how OpenAI's AI chatbot ChatGPT did not allow him to edit a photo of CEO Sam Altman, making him use Grok, the AI tool of Elon Musk 's xAI.Popular X users DogeDesigner recently shared a video from the Dreamforce 2025 event on social media platform. The video clip is an interview of Salesforce CEO Benioff from the event where he is seen saying that he turned to Grok to generate a viral image of himself alongside an AI-rendered Sam Altman, as ChatGPT rejected the request citing copyright risks from editing conference badges.The photo edit reportedly involved removing branded necklaces. Elon Musk replied to the post saying: Haha cool. Elon Musk's two-word reply to DogeDesigner's post has so far received 3,000-plus replies and 90,000-plus likes.What made his comments important is also hat so far Benioff has been ChatGPT's most vocal supporter. The same month (October 2025) Salesforce announced an expanded partnership with OpenAI, integrating its Agentforce 360 platform with ChatGPT and allowing enterprises to use OpenAI’s GPT-5 models within Salesforce products.At the time, Benioff praised that collaboration. “As consumers, we already get instant recommendations or insights from ChatGPT. ” “Now enterprises can deliver that same intelligence and immediacy,” he further said.In November 2025, Salesforce CEO publicly announced that he is abandoning OpenAI'schatbot after a brief trial of Google’s newly released Gemini 3 model. “Holy s—. I’ve used ChatGPT every day for 3 years. Just spent 2 hours on Gemini 3. I’m not going back,” Benioff wrote on X. “The leap is insane — reasoning, speed, images, video… everything is sharper and faster. It feels like the world just changed, again.”Incidentally, Dreamforce 2025 is also the event where the Salesforce CEO apologised for his statements where he said that President Donald Trump should make good on his threats to send the US national guard into San Francisco, despite resistance from local leaders. Benioff later said sorry, claiming he had “listened closely to my fellow San Franciscans and our local officials … I do not believe the national guard is needed to address safety in San Francisco. ” He added that Dreamforce’s security had been the main motivator for his statement. Benioff is a local power player and his company is the city’s largest private employer.
Article Summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff opened the company's marque AI conference Dreamforce 2025 hailing the "agentic enterprise" He also reportedly spoke about how OpenAI's AI chatbot ChatGPT did not allow him to edit a photo of CEO Sam Altman, making him use Grok, the AI tool of Elon Musk's xAI. In November 2025, Salesforce CEO publicly announced that he is abandoningChatGPT for Google’s newly released Gemini 3 model.

Article Title: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon : JP Morgan CEO who openly said he does not agree with 'work from home' says 4-day-week possible with AI as ...
Article Text:
JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon.

Long-term optimism, short-term risks

A “Wonderful Thing” for workers

Jamie Dimon on labour disruption

JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has long been vocal about his skepticism towards remote work, openly stating that he does not agree with the ‘work from home’ culture. He has consistently argued that in-person collaboration drives productivity and innovation. In a recent interview with Bloomberg TV, Dimon has struck a different tone when he discussed about the impact of artificial intelligence. He said that AI could eventually shorten the standard work week to four days, reshaping how businesses and employees operate. Dimon also elaborated that JPMorgan is already using AI across hundreds of applications, including fraud detection, risk management, marketing and error reduction.Dimon also predicted that in the next three to four decades, AI could boose productivity to the point where future generations might work four or event three-and-a-half-day weeks. However, he also cautioned that rapid deployment of AI could lead to job disruptions and layoffs before workers and industries fully adapt.Despite his reservations about remote work, Dimon described the prospect of a shorter work week enabled by AI as “a wonderful thing.”He emphasised that while the technology poses challenges, it also holds the potential to transform work-life balance and redefine the future of employment.Recently, Dimon urged the businesses and governments to start preparing for the labour disruption that artificial intelligence could bring. According to a report a Fortune, speaking at the company’s event, Dimon said that society should not wait until job losses materialise before addressing them. “I’m not predicting [it] can be a problem. I’m simply saying now’s the time to start thinking about what you do if it does,” he cautioned. Dimon further emphasises that JPMorgan is embracing AI transformation rather than ignoring it. The bank has already deployed a large language model used by 150,000 employees weekly, and Dimon also acknowledged that productivity gains from AI could mean the bank will employ fewer people in the next five years.
Article Summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has long been vocal about his skepticism towards remote work. He has consistently argued that in-person collaboration drives productivity and innovation. Dimon said that AI could eventually shorten the standard work week to four days. He also cautioned that rapid deployment of AI could lead to job disruptions and layoffs.

Article Title: Want to work at Facebook, WhatsApp or Instagram, Meta CTO has this ready-to-go guide
Article Text:
Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth

Communication and conflict resolution

'A Very Positive Case Study': Meta AI chief Alexandr Wang Praises India's AI Startup Ecosystem

Adaptability in a fast-changing workplace

Meta’s chief technology officer Andrew Bosworth, popularly known as Boz, recently took to Instagram for an AMA session. According to a report by Business Insider, when asked what type of person thrives at Meta — the parent company of Facebook , Instagram and WhatsApp . Replying to the query Bosworth offered a candid breakdown of the traits he values most in employees. Bosworth emphasised that the successful Meta employees are “relentless in pursuit of doing great work.” He described that the workers at Meta take pride in their contributions and treat their projects with a sense of personal ownership. As per him, this drive is what sets apart those who excel in the company’s fast-paced environment.Two of Bosworth’s tips are centred around communication. He said good employees are direct and appreciate directness in return, turning feedback into progress rather than conflict.He also acknowledged that direct communication can sometimes spark. He also said that good employees are direct and appreciate directness in return, turning feedback into progress rather than conflict. He also added that direct communication can sometimes spark disputes but pointed to his own framework for resolving workplace disagreements — a method he has used “so many times” that he even applies it to himself.Andrew Bosworth also highlighted adaptability as a crucial trait. “When plans change, their first reaction isn’t a knee-jerk fear of change, but rather a tremendous curiosity and enthusiasm about what that might mean for them,” he noted. With Meta frequently shifting priorities in areas like VR, AI, and the metaverse, employees who embrace change thrive.After listing technical and behavioral qualities, Bosworth concluded with something simple yet profound: “Just a good person.” For him, character matters as much as skill, and being trustworthy and ethical is non-negotiable.
Article Summary: Meta’s chief technology officer Andrew Bosworth, popularly known as Boz, recently took to Instagram for an AMA session. When asked what type of person thrives at Meta, Bosworth offered a candid breakdown of the traits he values most in employees.

Article Title: OpenAI VP leaves hours after Sam Altman's Pentagon deal, joins Anthropic; says: Many of people I most trust and respect have joined Anthropic over...
